name: Monaco
excerpt: The Genoese built a fortress on the site of present-day Monaco in 1215. The current ruling GRIMALDI family first seized control in 1297 but was not able to permanently secure its holding until 1419.

quick_facts.ethnic_groups: Monegasque 32.1%, French 19.9%, Italian 15.3%, British 5%, Belgian 2.3%, Swiss 2%, German 1.9%, Russian 1.8%, American 1.1%, Dutch 1.1%, Moroccan 1%, other 16.6% (2016 est.)
quick_facts.climate: Mediterranean with mild, wet winters and hot, dry summers
quick_facts.major_exports: jewelry, perfumes, watches, packaged medicines, cars (2021)

summaries.geography_summary: second-smallest independent state in the world (after the Holy See); smallest country with a coastline; almost entirely urban
summaries.history_summary: The Genoese built a fortress on the site of present-day Monaco in 1215. The current ruling GRIMALDI family first seized control in 1297 but was not able to permanently secure its holding until 1419. Economic development was spurred in the late 19th century with a railroad linkup to France and the opening of a casino. Since then, the principality's mild climate, coastal Mediterranean scenery, and gambling facilities have made Monaco world-famous as a tourist and recreation center.
summaries.government_summary: constitutional monarchy. Independence: 1419 (beginning of permanent rule by the House of GRIMALDI)
summaries.economy_summary: high-income European economy; non-EU euro user; considered a tax haven; tourism and banking are largest sectors; negatively impacted by COVID-19; major oceanographic museum; among most expensive real estate; major state-owned enterprises

suggested_reading: CIA World Factbook data (archived via factbook.json) | https://github.com/factbook/factbook.json
